beats.activity: Wake up, feed Koji, grab coffee on the porch.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus is a morning person with a steady routine; yesterday's energy was moderate-to-good, so he's up early. Creature of habit—coffee, dog care, settle the mind before work.

beats.activity: Drive to Sea-Tac, beat the morning rush.
beats.reasoning: Commute from Ballard to Sea-Tac; he prefers leaving early to avoid traffic. Tower shift typically starts around 08:00.

beats.activity: Work shift in the tower—steady morning flow, clear skies helping traffic move.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift at Sea-Tac; clear skies and good visibility mean efficient operations. Air quality is moderate due to distant smoke, but not impairing tower operations.

beats.activity: Lunch at the Sea-Tac café—sandwich, water, stay hydrated in the heat.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus eats at his desk or the café most days; today's heat (91F forecast) makes hydration a priority. He's frugal and routine—same sandwich spot.

beats.activity: Afternoon tower shift—traffic building as heat peaks; smoke haze noticeable but no restrictions.
beats.reasoning: Second half of shift. Afternoon heat doesn't affect tower operations, but Cyrus is aware of air quality. He'll clock out by 16:30 and head home to prep for evening.

beats.activity: Drive home to Ballard; stop at QFC for supplies.
beats.reasoning: Quick errand on the way home—he's frugal and meal-plans. Stops at the neighborhood QFC rather than a fancier store. Home by 17:15 to avoid evening crowds.

beats.activity: Home, drop groceries, change into shorts and running gear; brief water break.
beats.reasoning: He's a trail runner and climber; the clear, hot evening is perfect for exercise. He'll hydrate and change quickly—this is his favorite time of day.

beats.activity: Trail run at Discovery Park—long easy run in the heat, cool down on the water overlook.
beats.reasoning: Clear skies and warm evening—his ideal conditions. Discovery Park is a short drive from Ballard and his favorite spot. He'll time it to finish before the 6pm concert crowd builds, though he's not interested in the concert itself (values quiet over crowds).

beats.activity: Drive back to Ballard; shower and change into fresh clothes.
beats.reasoning: Returns home after run; cleans up before evening. The Gas Works Park concert is happening now, but Cyrus avoids big crowds—he values solitude and close friends over public events.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a simple dinner on the porch with Koji; read a book as light fades.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus winds down with his familiar routine: quiet dinner, dog company, a book. The evening is still warm and clear. No interest in the concert crowd; he prefers peace and solitude.

beats.activity: Inside, tidy up kitchen, prep tomorrow's gear, settle with Koji for the evening.
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down at home before bed. He's methodical and frugal—preps his work bag and climbing gear for tomorrow, lets his mind settle.
Solid 8.5-hour sleep; Cyrus is disciplined and consistent. Early riser tomorrow, so lights out by 21:30.

diary: Woke early with Koji demanding breakfast—he's got an internal clock better than any radar I use at the tower. Coffee on the porch was perfect; the heat was already building by 7 AM. Work was smooth this morning, clear skies made everything feel manageable, though by afternoon the smoke haze rolled in from BC and things got busier. Nothing scary, just that steady hum of concentration you get when traffic stacks up and the heat makes everyone want to fly somewhere cooler. Grabbed lunch, stayed hydrated like I always do. After my shift I hit Discovery Park for a run—needed to burn off the day's tension. The trail was quiet, and standing on the overlook afterward, looking out at the sound, I remembered why I love this city even when it's sweltering. Back home now with Koji on the porch, dinner done, the light going soft. Heard the concert starting over at Gas Works but I'm content right here. Tired in a good way.
thought_process: The heat made me deliberate about hydration and pacing—I've learned after decades in the tower that taking care of my body directly affects my focus. The smoke was a minor concern professionally, but nothing that changed operations. I chose the run partly to decompress from the afternoon rush, partly because the weather invited it. I skipped the concert; at 57, crowds and noise don't appeal like they used to. Instead I gravitated toward quiet ritual—Koji, the porch, a book. The day felt balanced between responsibility and rest, which matters more now than it did twenty years ago.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
